name: Port of Cabigordo and Barony of Escriche – circular from Cedrillas
name_original: Puerto de Cabigordo y Baronía de Escriche – circular desde Cedrillas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cedrillas features routes through mountainous terrain, characterized by significant elevation changes and expansive views. The region includes areas like Valdelinares and the Barony of Escriche, offering varied landscapes for cyclists. Many routes traverse open plains and mountain passes, providing a challenging experience. The area's geography is well-suited for cyclists seeking demanding climbs and descents. Best touring cycling routes around Cedrillas The most popular touring cycling route is Empty Mountains – circular by Valdelinares from Virgen de la Vega via Linares de la Mora, a 23.5 miles (37.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive mountain views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Port of Cabigordo and Barony of Escriche – circular from Cedrillas, a difficult 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route passes through varied landscapes, including the Port of Cabigordo. Local touring cyclists also love the Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop from Formiche Alto, a 66.2 miles (106.6 km) trail leading through open plains, often completed in about 7 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Cedrillas is defined by mountain passes, open plains, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for moderate to advanced cyclists. The routes in Cedrillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cedrillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cedrillas?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Cedrillas, offering a variety of experiences through mountainous terrain and open plains. The region is known for its significant elevation changes, providing a challenging experience for cyclists.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cedrillas?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cedrillas are generally quite demanding. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy', you can find 5 routes rated as 'moderate' difficulty. The majority of the 27 routes are considered 'difficult', featuring substantial climbs and descents.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Cedrillas area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Cedrillas offers numerous difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop from Formiche Alto&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 66.2 miles (106.6 km) with over 1500 meters of elevation gain, often taking more than 7 hours to complete. This route leads through expansive open plains.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Cedrillas?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the Cedrillas area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955765456/empty-mountains-circular-by-valdelinares-from-virgen-de-la-vega-via-linares-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains – circular by Valdelinares from Virgen de la Vega via Linares de la Mora&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.5-mile (37.8 km) difficult route offering extensive mountain views.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes near Cedrillas?
A: Touring cycling around Cedrillas offers diverse scenery, from expansive open plains to rugged mountain passes. You'll encounter areas like Valdelinares and the Barony of Escriche, characterized by significant elevation changes and panoramic views. Many routes provide a sense of remoteness, often referred to as 'empty mountains'.

Q: Are there any historical sites or natural landmarks to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the Cedrillas area is rich in natural and historical points of interest. You might pass by landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cabigordo top of the col sign&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;. Natural formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2381633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ojerada Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt; and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castellar Dinosaur Tracksite&lt;/a&gt; are also within the broader region.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cedrillas?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for significant elevation, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Cedrillas. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring snow and colder temperatures, especially at higher altitudes like Valdelinares.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring bikes at Cedrillas trailheads?
A: Many touring cycling routes around Cedrillas start from villages or specific points like Virgen de la Vega or Cedrillas itself. Parking availability will vary by starting point. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on recommended parking areas near the trailhead.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Cedrillas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cedrillas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the extensive mountain views, and the sense of adventure found in the varied landscapes, including areas like the Barony of Escriche.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through the Valdelinares area?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes explore the Valdelinares area, known for its mountainous characteristics. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega – Puerto de Valdelinares loop from Urbanización Solano de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.9 km route that takes you through this scenic region, featuring significant climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Port of Cabigordo?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955848193/port-of-cabigordo-and-barony-of-escriche-circular-from-cedrillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Cabigordo and Barony of Escriche – circular from Cedrillas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.7-mile (34.9 km) route that specifically passes through the Port of Cabigordo, offering cyclists views of this notable mountain pass.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Barony of Escriche?
A: Yes, the Barony of Escriche is a prominent feature in the Cedrillas touring cycling landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955848193/port-of-cabigordo-and-barony-of-escriche-circular-from-cedrillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Cabigordo and Barony of Escriche – circular from Cedrillas&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience this area, combining it with the challenge of the Port of Cabigordo.
tips: The barony of Escriche was a small town during the 16th-17th centuries, owned by the Baron of Escriche, where 14 farmhouses were located. The most representative is the palace and the church of San Bartolomé. An ideal area for mountain bike lovers.
Road in good condition but very hot in summer and cold in winter, with the occasional chance of snow at the top. There are no fountains for a long stretch so stock up well before heading out.
Village with services: accommodation, bars, restaurants.
